calendar.matters:
DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Oct-19-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. KENNETH P. NABITY, ESQ. of Kerr & Wagstaff LLP, Tel No. 415-371-8500 appearing for Respondents Aechelon Technology, Inc., Luis Barcena, Cristopher Blumenthal, Javier Castellar and Ignacio Sanz-Pastor;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Respondents Aechelon Technology, Inc., Luis Barcena, Cristopher Blumenthal, Javier Castellar and Ignacio Sanz-Pastor's Demurrer for Writ of Mandate for Issuance of Stock Certificates is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. Corporations Code Section 416 entitles share holders to a certificate in their name certifying their ownership of shares in a corporation. In 1998, Aechelon Technology, Inc. issued Quinn a certificate. Quinn concedes that he lost the certificate. In 2015, Aechelon Technology, Inc. found the certificate in a safety deposit box and returned it to Quinn. Aechelon also sent new share certificates to Quinn and Quinn's son. Corporations Code section 2201 provides that a corporate officer is personally liable if he breaches his duty to transfer shares. Every director or other officer who unreasonably causes such neglect or refusal to enter such transfers is also personally liable. (Ibid.) In the instant case, Respondents merely asked Quinn to sign a standard Stock Transfer Agreement. A casual email alone did not need to suffice. Nonetheless, Respondents issued Quinn and his son new certificates either in goodwill or in an effort to thwart the instant lawsuit. It is not unreasonable to say that doing so was successful. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
